Workout Sheets — R1

The reference sheet: bottom sheet in the card language, grabber, 10px top radii, the logging screen dimmed beneath a neutral scrim. Frame 1 is the Adjust sheet — weight is opened pre-filled at the auto-progression's accepted 100 lb (from the RPE-suggestion moment upstream in this wave), fine-tuned via ≥44pt steppers that honor the exercise's weight_increment. Frame 2 is Add Exercise with the keyboard up (#67): the search field and its result rows sit entirely above the keyboard's blocked-out region — never behind it. Judge: does this sheet read as the one modal pattern the whole app should inherit, and is the keyboard law unmistakable at a glance.

Chest
Incline Bench Press
Incline Dumbbell Press
Incline Cable Fly
System keyboard — blocked out
Add exercise · keyboard up (#67)

This is the one sheet pattern — grabber, 10px top radii, card-top fill, neutral scrim — every later modal in APEX inherits rather than reinventing its own chrome. Frame 1's steppers move in the exercise's own weight_increment (shown here at its 5 lb default); the toggle and Apply stay structural and accent respectively, nothing is colored without a reason. Frame 2 states the #67 law literally: the search field and every result row it can show stay fully above the keyboard's blocked-out region — this anchoring binds every screen in the app that raises a keyboard, not just this one.
